homepage.heroSubline: Honest diagnostics and reliable repairs for every make and model. Serving Memphis drivers since 2010 with factory-trained technicians.
aboutText: Founded in a small garage near Elvis Presley Blvd, Horizon Auto Repair has grown into one of Memphis' most trusted auto service centers. We started with just two lifts and a commitment to fair pricing.

Today, we combine old-school mechanical expertise with the latest diagnostic technology. Our team specializes in domestic and import vehicles commonly seen on Memphis roads, from Chevy trucks to Nissan sedans.
We're proud to support local charities like the Mid-South Food Bank and sponsor youth sports teams in Shelby County.

valueProps.title: No Upselling
valueProps.description: We recommend only what your vehicle truly needs - no unnecessary repairs.

valueProps.title: Loaner Cars
valueProps.description: Free loaner vehicles available for most major repairs over 4 hours.

valueProps.title: 3-Year Warranty
valueProps.description: All parts and labor backed by our industry-leading warranty.

valueProps.title: Local Parts Network
valueProps.description: Direct relationships with Memphis-area suppliers mean faster repairs and better pricing.

homepage.ctaSubline: Same-day appointments available for most services. Call or book online.

services.name: Transmission Services
services.shortDescription: Expert diagnosis and repair for automatic and manual transmissions.
services.description:
Memphis' stop-and-go traffic on I-240 and I-55 puts extra strain on transmissions. Our technicians perform complete transmission diagnostics using the latest scanner technology to pinpoint issues accurately. We specialize in:

Rebuilding worn transmissions rather than pushing unnecessary replacements
Fluid exchanges to prevent Memphis' summer heat from breaking down your transmission
Computerized adjustments for shifting problems common in Fords and Chevrolets

For older vehicles common in Memphis' historic neighborhoods, we source quality rebuilt units from local suppliers when repairs aren't feasible. All work includes a free 20-mile test drive through downtown traffic to verify repairs.
process: Step 1: Road test and computer diagnostics
Step 2: Detailed estimate with repair options
Step 3: Disassembly and component inspection
Step 4: Rebuild or replace with test drive verification
benefits: Extends transmission life 2-3x longer than quick fixes
Prevents breakdowns on busy Memphis interstates
Includes free fluid top-offs for 6 months

faq.question: How often should I service my transmission?
faq.answer: We recommend fluid changes every 30,000 miles for most Memphis drivers. Heavy stop-and-go traffic may require more frequent service.

faq.question: What are signs my transmission is failing?
faq.answer: Delayed shifting, slipping gears, and fluid leaks are common warnings. Don't wait until you're stuck on Summer Avenue - bring your car in at the first sign of trouble.

faq.question: Can you work on European transmissions?
faq.answer: Yes, our lead technician is certified on BMW, Mercedes, and Audi transmissions commonly found in East Memphis neighborhoods.

services.name: Brake Services
services.shortDescription: Complete brake inspections and repairs for Memphis driving conditions.
services.description:
From the hills of Cordova to downtown's rainy streets, Memphis demands reliable brakes. We perform comprehensive brake services using premium pads and rotors that stand up to Tennessee's variable weather. Our process includes:

Measuring remaining pad thickness with digital calipers (most shops just eyeball it)
Resurfacing rotors when possible to save you money
Bleeding brake lines to remove moisture that causes spongy pedals
Checking caliper slides that often stick in our humid climate

We stock brake parts for common Memphis vehicles like Toyota Camrys and Ford F-150s, with same-day service available for most repairs. All brake work includes a free retorque after 50 miles to ensure everything stays tight.
process: Step 1: Test drive and visual inspection
Step 2: Measurement of all brake components
Step 3: Customer-approved repair plan
Step 4: Quality parts installation and road test
benefits: Quieter stops than cheap chain-store brake jobs
Ceramic pads available for less brake dust

faq.question: How often should brakes be inspected?
faq.answer: Every oil change for Memphis drivers. Our salty winter roads and summer downpours accelerate brake wear.

faq.question: Why do my brakes squeak after repair?
faq.answer: Some noise during the 200-mile break-in period is normal. Persistent squeaks mean we'll adjust them at no charge.

faq.question: Do you work on anti-lock brake systems?
faq.answer: Yes, we're equipped to diagnose and repair ABS issues that trigger warning lights on your dashboard.

services.name: AC Repair
services.shortDescription: Complete air conditioning service for Memphis' humid summers.
services.description:
When temperatures hit 95°F with 80% humidity, you need reliable AC. Our certified technicians perform thorough diagnostics using manifold gauges and leak detectors to find problems other shops miss. We specialize in:

Identifying refrigerant leaks with UV dye (common in older cars on Memphis roads)
Replacing expansion valves clogged by Southern pollen
Repairing blend door actuators that fail in our temperature swings

For classic cars seen at Memphis car shows, we offer R-12 conversions to modern refrigerants. All AC repairs include a free recharge if needed within 90 days and a cabin air filter check to keep Memphis allergens out of your vents.
process: Step 1: Performance test and pressure readings
Step 2: Leak detection and component inspection
Step 3: Repair approval with upfront pricing
Step 4: System evacuation and recharge
benefits: Proper oil charge prevents compressor failure

faq.question: Why does my AC stop working at red lights?
faq.answer: This usually indicates a failing condenser fan. We test these under actual Memphis driving conditions.

faq.question: How often should AC systems be serviced?
faq.answer: Annual checkups are wise. Our humidity causes mold growth that clogs systems.

faq.question: Can you fix electrical AC problems?
faq.answer: Yes, we diagnose wiring issues and faulty relays common in older Memphis vehicles.

services.name: Engine Diagnostics
services.shortDescription: Advanced computer diagnostics for check engine lights and drivability issues.
services.description:
Modern vehicles have up to 50 computers controlling engine performance. Our $25,000 diagnostic setup reads all manufacturer-specific codes, not just generic OBD2 data. This lets us accurately diagnose:

Misfires caused by Memphis' ethanol-blended fuels
Faulty oxygen sensors common in high-mileage cars
Timing chain issues in certain Nissan and Honda models

We maintain factory service information for all major makes, so we don't just guess at repairs. For intermittent problems, we offer extended test drives replicating Memphis driving conditions to reproduce hard-to-find issues. All diagnostics include a detailed printed report explaining the problem in plain English.
process: Step 1: Full-system scan with OEM software
Step 2: Live data monitoring during test drive
Step 3: Component testing beyond code reading
Step 4: Repair options with cost/benefit analysis
benefits: Identifies root causes, not just symptoms
Includes free retest if light returns
Detailed reports help with resale value

faq.question: Why is my check engine light on after fuel fill-up?
faq.answer: This often indicates a loose gas cap - we check this for free before any charges.

faq.question: Can you diagnose transmission codes too?
faq.answer: Yes, our scanners read all modules including transmission, ABS, and airbag systems.

faq.question: How accurate are your diagnostics?
faq.answer: We achieve 98% first-time accuracy by combining scans with mechanical inspections.

services.name: Suspension Services
services.shortDescription: Complete suspension repairs that survive Memphis potholes.
services.description:
From potholes on Poplar Avenue to rough railroad crossings, Memphis roads test every suspension component. We perform comprehensive inspections measuring:

Strut/shock absorber performance (replaced in pairs for even handling)
Ball joint and control arm wear (common in trucks used on country roads)
Bushing deterioration from Tennessee's heat cycles

We use laser alignment equipment to precisely set angles after repairs, crucial for preventing tire wear on Memphis' crowned roads. For luxury vehicles in East Memphis, we offer factory-spec alignments using OEM tolerance charts. All suspension work includes a free alignment check after 500 miles.
process: Step 1: Road test and bounce test
Step 2: Lift inspection with measurement tools
Step 3: Customer-approved repair plan
Step 4: Precision alignment post-repair

faq.question: Why does my car pull after hitting a pothole?
faq.answer: This usually indicates bent steering components. We check tie rods and control arms thoroughly.

faq.question: How often should shocks be replaced?
faq.answer: Every 50,000 miles is typical for Memphis driving. Worn shocks accelerate other suspension wear.

faq.question: Do you align lifted trucks?
faq.answer: Yes, we properly align trucks with up to 4" lifts common in our area.

reviews.name: Marcus J.
reviews.text: After two shops told me I needed a $4,000 replacement, Horizon diagnosed a bad solenoid pack. $600 repair fixed my shifting issues completely. They even showed me the faulty part and explained how the repair works. My truck shifts better than it has in years.

reviews.name: Lisa T.
reviews.text: My AC would only blow cold on the highway - turns out the condenser fan was failing. Horizon found the issue quickly and had me back on the road same day. The tech took time to show me the melted wiring that caused the problem. Great service in this Memphis heat!

reviews.name: Darnell W.
reviews.text: Finally found a shop that doesn't try to upsell rotors when they can be resurfaced. My brakes are silent now and stop perfectly, even in rainy downtown traffic. Appreciate the honest assessment and quality workmanship.

reviews.name: Sarah K.
reviews.text: My Subaru had an intermittent misfire three other shops couldn't replicate. Horizon's technician rode with me to pinpoint the issue - turned out to be a wiring harness rubbing against the engine. Detailed invoice showed exactly what was fixed. Will never go anywhere else.

reviews.name: James P.
reviews.text: Had a horrible clunking noise over bumps. Horizon replaced both front struts and control arm bushings, then did a perfect alignment. Car drives like new now, even on those rough streets near the Medical District. Fair price and no hidden fees.

reviews.name: Elena R.
reviews.text: Paid for their thorough 125-point inspection before buying a used Tahoe. They found $2,800 in needed repairs the dealer 'forgot' to mention. Negotiated the repair cost off the price and had Horizon do the work. Saved me from a bad purchase!

Our skilled technicians specialize in everything from routine maintenance to complex repairs, offering reliable service with a personal touch.
